zh.lifeSummary: 當Marguerite Geneviève de Thouars於1125年出生在法國波特-哈倫斯多武-瑟艾福雷斯瓦爾斯時，她的父親Aimery VI De Thouars30歲，母親Mathilde d'Aquitaine de Poitiers22歲。她和Bellay IV de Montreuil至少有1個兒子。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Marguerite
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of Margaret , also used in the English-speaking world, where its use has been reinforced by the fact that the name was adopted in the 19th century for a garden flower, a large cultivated variety of daisy. Margaret was earlier used in English as a dialect word denoting the ox-eye daisy, and the French equivalent was borrowed into English just in time to catch the vogue for deriving girls' names from vocabulary words denoting flowers. See also Daisy .

nameMeanings.name: Geneviève
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The name of the patron saint of Paris, Geneviève in French. She was a 5th-century Gallo-Roman nun who encouraged the people of Paris in the face of the occupation of the city by the Franks and threatened attacks by the Huns. Her name is probably derived from Celtic words meaning ‘people, tribe’ and ‘woman’, but if so it has been heavily altered during its transmission through French sources. The name was introduced to Britain from France in the 19th century and is now in steady use.

en.lifeSummary: When Marguerite Geneviève de Thouars was born in 1125, in Thouars, Deux-Sèvres, Poitou-Charentes, France, her father, Aimery VI De Thouars, was 30 and her mother, Mathilde d'Aquitaine de Poitiers, was 22. She had at least 1 son with Bellay IV de Montreuil.
